context: Tests whether the agent decorates all gameplay Schema fields with @type, uses MapSchema<Player> keyed by sessionId, and correctly implements the four required lifecycle hooks. A naive implementation might use plain TypeScript class properties — the skill specifically warns against this for any client-visible state.

checklist.name: HP field decorated
checklist.description: Player health/hp field uses @type decorator (not a plain class property)

checklist.name: Position fields decorated
checklist.description: Player x and y fields use @type decorator

checklist.name: MapSchema for players
checklist.description: BattleState uses MapSchema<Player> (not an array or plain object) for the players collection

checklist.name: sessionId as map key
checklist.description: Players are stored in the map using client.sessionId as the key

checklist.name: onCreate sets state
checklist.description: onCreate calls this.setState() with a new BattleState instance

checklist.name: onJoin adds player
checklist.description: onJoin creates a Player and adds it to state.players with sessionId as key

checklist.name: onLeave removes player
checklist.description: onLeave removes the player from state.players using client.sessionId

checklist.name: All four hooks present
checklist.description: File contains onCreate, onJoin, onLeave, and onDispose methods
